scope.target: Rebase the workflow branch onto main and revalidate the repository pattern analysis against the updated validator architecture
scope.dependency_radius: Repository decisions, validator ownership, Roslyn analyzer scope, transitional script retirement, and final validation
constraints: Preserve the committed workflow bootstrap during rebase
Use rebase-updated main as the new evidence baseline
Do not resolve user architecture decisions
Do not modify canonical repository standards in this task

inputs.architecture_target: A rebase-aware remediation plan that accounts for the completed .NET Validator Phase 3 work
review_findings: Main now explicitly treats the repository grep script as a non-gating transitional asset
DBA1001 already provides bootstrap semantic validation for repository query methods
DBA1001 currently encodes the five-method repository policy and uses a broad repository-name heuristic
Validator Phase 3 intentionally deferred repository policy to this workflow
user_constraints: Repeat the analysis and revise the plan before user decisions

steps: Created the workflow branch and committed the existing workflow artifacts
Rebased the workflow branch onto local main
Compared the old baseline with main
Reviewed Validator Phase 3 requirements, workflow, script ownership, projection standards, and DBA1001
Updated the repository workflow plan and review report
validation: Parse all workflow task JSON files
Confirm the workflow remains awaiting user decisions
deferred_items: Implement canonical standards and validator changes

results.summary: Created and committed the workflow branch, rebased it onto local main without conflicts, revalidated the repository analysis against completed Validator Phase 3 work, retained D1-D9, added D10, and changed S4 from grep-script repair to DBA1001 alignment plus script retirement.
tests_run: Parsed all workflow task JSON files with PowerShell ConvertFrom-Json
Validated local Markdown links in workflow-plan.md and review-report.md
